title: Pilosella iserana
intro: Pilosella ierana, also known as a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, is a rare species native to the mountainous regions of Albania. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella ierana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pilosella ierana is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Pilosella iserana is a species within the genus Pilosella, which is a group of flowering plants in the tribe Cichorieae, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other dandelion-like species. The Asteraceae family includes many diverse plants, such as sunflowers and daisies, which also share similar characteristics. The genus Pilosella is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics and distributions.
